text:
When I wrote my first post on the emerging role of smart data in business back in 2013, my friends and family had to remind me to take out the trash, or that the back door was unlocked, or even to wish my mom a “Happy Mother’s Day!” Now, four years later and with all the advancements we’re seeing in IoT (Internet of Things) technologies, the pace of global sensor deployment is still accelerating — and by a factor that’s expected to grow. All those sensors will be producing enormous volumes of “stuff” which means that the real challenge is turning that “stuff” into useful information. The fact is, turning all that sensor generated data into something meaningful will require many new skills, especially related to data-in-context or what I’ve chosen to call “smart data”: data acquisition, data modeling, data reasoning and finally, data dissemination and presentation.

Sensoring and pervasive systems are considered among the key technologies to provide means to cope with the deluge of available data today. This paper presents a survey on context awareness that takes into account the Internet of Things (IoT) context. We start by introducing briefly the main principles of IoT and context awareness, by presenting with great details the context life cycle. By the use of a specialized taxonomy, we analyze a set of fifty works (research and industrial) implemented in the context of IoT from 2001 to 2011. The main goal of this paper is providing an overview of ten years of research through the evaluation and analysis of the different approaches, methods and middlewares that have been proposed in the existing literature; ultimately providing insights on the applicability of such contributions to the emerging IoT paradigm.
